summary: Audience cassette with left‑channel quirks captures a high‑energy July 11 1970 Fillmore East show – stellar acoustic set and thundering Morning Dew despite crude audio.
review: The performance is widely praised for its raw power: an acoustic half featuring the rare Everly Brothers cover “So Sad (To Watch Good Love Go Bad)”, “Rosa Lee” and three gospel numbers, followed by a blistering electric set that opens with “Easy Wind” and rockets into a soaring “Morning Dew”.  The recording is an audience cassette from the stone‑age era – left channel only for the first nine songs, tape hiss and occasional silence – but listeners consider it good enough given its historical value.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the music 4‑5 stars and highlight standout moments, while recording quality averages around fair (3‑4 stars). Weighted combination yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Left channel carries the first ~9 songs; right channel is mostly hiss until the acoustic “A Voice From On High”. Tape hiss, occasional silence and overall cassette‑generation noise. No soundboard source; listeners note it’s crude but usable for historical purposes.

standout_songs: So Sad (To Watch Good Love Go Bad)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic half opens with rare Everly Brothers cover and three gospel numbers, then transitions to the electric set featuring Easy Wind, a Dancing‑Cryptical‑Other One medley and culminates in an explosive Morning Dew.  The energy builds throughout, highlighted by Jerry’s pedal‑steel work and New Riders’ presence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for abundant guitar/pedal‑steel work, high energy solos, possible early wah‑wah use.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides vocals on “Good Lovin'” and joins chorus on the line "out where the horses were tied"; vocal contributions noted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen (keyboard) plays throughout, even delivering a solo set despite laryngitis; keyboards are audible but not front‑mix.
